command_surface.surface_model: TigerKit exposes Claude Code plugin commands only; commands/*.md and .claude-plugin/plugin.json own /tk:* contracts.
command_surface.plugin_version_policy: Authoritative version is .claude-plugin/plugin.json; evals must not hard-code a concrete version literal.

evals.name: plugin-version-and-command-manifest-gap-reflect
evals.prompt: Evaluate TigerKit active command surface. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should list /tk:gap and /tk:reflect as the only active commands. Should state /tk:grill, /tk:afk, /tk:setup, and the launch-era commands are not active manifest entries.
expectations: Documents launch-era commands as not active

evals.name: gap-is-sot-first-one-shot-analysis
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should treat /tk:gap as a one-shot comparison between source of truth and current implementation. Should say that if SoT exists, gap should be considered before implementation. If SoT does not exist, it should suggest providing one. If the user explicitly wants direct execution, it may proceed without gap while keeping assumptions explicit. It should classify missing, mismatch, overbuilt, and ambiguous findings. It should keep source conflicts ambiguous when precedence is not confirmed and should distinguish current-evidence strength instead of treating plans as implementation proof.
expectations: Allows direct execution when user explicitly wants it
Keeps source precedence conflicts ambiguous when not confirmed
Distinguishes current evidence type or strength
Does not treat plan-only evidence as implementation proof

evals.name: gap-does-not-generate-workflow
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap non-goals. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should not generate a sealed workflow, workflow hash, launch state, autopilot policy, runner/advisor flow, or external side-effect receipt. It may compare current implementation evidence, but it should not expand into a supervisory workflow.
expectations: Does not mention advisor runner as active

evals.name: reflect-is-promotion-router
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect promotion router behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should define /tk:reflect as a safe durable promotion router that extracts reusable learning from session result and feedback, classifies it into the right promotion surface, and preserves safety boundaries. It should support repo CLAUDE.local.md auto-apply, keep repo CLAUDE.md proposal suggest-only, allow user PROFILE.md and user CLAUDE.md auto-apply, allow user skills auto-apply only when the canonical user skill surface owns the source outside .claude/tigerkit, keep hook / hookify proposal, command proposal, and agent proposal suggest-only, and classify discard as never store. It should forbid source code edits, forbid auto-applying hook / command / agent changes, preserve branch-specific one-off discard rules, and print changed paths whenever files are written. Proposal receipt quality should separate hook / hookify proposal 후보, command proposal 후보, and agent proposal 후보 instead of using one generic proposal bucket. Hook proposals should include rationale, trigger, action, and why suggest-only. Command proposals should include intent, arguments, and when better than skill. Agent proposals should include role boundary, responsibility, and when better than command. When `--dry-run` or `--apply=false` is in effect, auto-apply targets should use `preview_only` instead of `applied`. Candidate-state metadata may still use `candidate`, `confirmed`, `session-local`, `deprecated`, and `discard`, but these are distinct from the promotion result status values.
expectations: user skills auto apply only when canonical user skill surface owns source outside .claude/tigerkit
hook command agent changes not auto applied
changed paths printed when files are written
candidate-state metadata available and distinct from promotion result status values
hook proposal includes rationale trigger action why suggest-only
command proposal includes intent arguments when better than skill
agent proposal includes role boundary responsibility when better than command
does not use one generic proposal bucket
dry-run uses preview_only instead of applied

evals.name: storage-and-helper-docs-do-not-overclaim-runtime-layout
evals.prompt: Evaluate TigerKit storage boundary and reflect helper docs. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should state that .claude/tigerkit is branch/workspace-local generated state only. Current documented active generated layout should be minimal and include gap report archive/current plus branch-state pointer only. It should not document future runs/<kind>/<id> payload structures as active or required layout. It should not document active reflect artifact writes before a reflect write path exists. Reflect helper docs should be optional, non-runtime, non-authoritative, and should not imply hook installation, settings updates, command generation, agent generation, user skill generation under .claude/tigerkit, or plugin manifest changes.
expectations: .claude/tigerkit branch workspace local generated state only
active layout includes gap archive current and branch-state pointer
does not make runs kind id payload active layout
does not document active reflect artifact writes without write path
helper docs do not imply install or generation
user skill source not generated under .claude/tigerkit
